summary: iter-105.24 frontend-only — manual_only flow for Cura + Flash Studio Desktop verified end-to-end via Playwright on the live preview URL, with regression on OrcaSlicer + PrusaSlicer auto-launch path intact. 6/6 review items PASS. (1) Bundle smoke on /static/js/bundle.js: noProtocolLauncher × 6, manual_only × 2, orca-launch-manual-only × 1, orca-launch-copy-filename-manual-btn × 1 — all required strings shipped. (6) Direct Node import of BUILTIN_SLICERS: 9/9 assertions PASS — exactly 7 builtins, cura.noProtocolLauncher === true, flashstudio.noProtocolLauncher === true, orcaslicer/orca-flashforge/bambu-studio/prusaslicer/superslicer all undefined. (2,3) Cura + Flash Studio manual_only: download triggered → orca-launch-manual-only card visible, orca-launch-uncertain/likely NOT visible, orca-launch-copy-filename-manual-btn present, description reads 'doesn\'t support browser auto-launch — you\'ll open the file manually', ZERO POSTs to /api/exports/handoff (handoff staging correctly skipped). (4,5) OrcaSlicer + PrusaSlicer regression: download triggered, orca-launch-uncertain card shown (manual_only NOT shown), exactly 1 POST to /api/exports/handoff per launch — iter-105.23 stageHandoff() path intact. Description shows 'tries to launch it via its <protocol>:// handler'. Headless Chromium doesn't surface window.location.href=cura://... navigations to anchor-traps, but per the review note (other_misc_info) the testid presence + handoff-call absence are together sufficient proof that no URL-protocol launch is attempted for Cura/FlashStudio.
critical_code_review_comments: OrcaDialog.handleDownload short-circuit (lines 109-117) is correct: setLaunchState('manual_only') + toast.info + setBusy(false) + return — does NOT call stageHandoff or launchSlicer for noProtocolLauncher slicers. Verified empirically by zero /api/exports/handoff network requests for Cura/FlashStudio.
Dialog description (lines 236-253) conditionally shows the manual warning copy when slicer.noProtocolLauncher is true — distinct strings for the two paths, verified via DOM text-content scrape.

Manual-only card (lines 303-342) mirrors the uncertain card UX (filename code box + copy button + default-app tip) but with sky-blue chrome and honest copy. Both filename-display testids (orca-launch-filename-manual, orca-launch-copy-filename-manual-btn) present and unique vs. the amber uncertain-card variants.

BUILTIN_SLICERS schema: noProtocolLauncher is an additive optional field — backwards-compatible with the rest of customSlicers.js (loadCustomSlicers filter doesn't touch it, getAllSlicers passes it through). User-custom slicers via addCustomSlicer() don't get the flag (correct — they're presumed to register a real protocol).

launchSlicer() itself was NOT modified for noProtocolLauncher slicers in iter-105.24 — the short-circuit lives in the dialog. This means a future caller could still bypass and call launchSlicer('cura://') directly. Minor architectural nit (could move the guard into launchSlicer to make it bulletproof), not a bug — the only caller today is OrcaDialog.
Toast duration 12000ms is appropriate for the manual_only instructions (text is dense — 3 sentences).

success_rate.frontend: 100% (6/6 review items PASS: 4 Playwright slicer flows + bundle smoke + BUILTIN_SLICERS unit)

seed_data_creation: Ephemeral: each Playwright iteration added a single Cube primitive via add-primitive-btn → add-primitive-cube, then exported a .3mf for download. No persistent server state created (OrcaSlicer/Prusa staged handoffs are single-shot + 30 min TTL).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Canonical regression: (a) curl bundle.js and grep for noProtocolLauncher + manual_only + orca-launch-manual-only (must be ≥1 each). (b) `node /tmp/check_slicers.mjs` from /app/frontend dir for the BUILTIN_SLICERS unit check (9/9). (c) For Playwright, IMPORTANT: dismiss the 'OK, got it' announcement modal AND the 'Got it' auto-theme toast before interacting — splash-screen overlay intercepts pointer events otherwise. Then add a cube via add-primitive-btn → add-primitive-cube, open send-to-orcaslicer-btn (which opens orca-dialog), switch slicer via orca-slicer-select dropdown by label, click orca-download-btn, expect a download event within 10s, then assert testid presence of orca-launch-manual-only (Cura/FlashStudio) OR orca-launch-uncertain (Orca/Prusa). Track network for /api/exports/handoff — expect 0 calls for manual_only slicers, ≥1 for auto-launch slicers. Headless Chromium does NOT surface window.location.href=cura:// navigations to page.on('framenavigated') or anchor-traps; rely on testid presence + handoff-call count as the proof of correctness instead.
rca_of_the_issue: Original bug (user-reported iter-105.24): Cura + Flash Studio appeared to do nothing when selected as the target slicer, because previous code unconditionally called launchSlicer() which set window.location.href to 'cura://...' / 'flashforge://...' — neither vendor registers an OS URL-protocol handler, so the OS swallowed it silently. iter-105.24 fix verified working: source-of-truth flag BUILTIN_SLICERS[*].noProtocolLauncher === true on cura + flashstudio entries; OrcaDialog.handleDownload short-circuits on this flag — downloads the .3mf locally, shows the sky-blue orca-launch-manual-only card with explicit 'open <Slicer> and drag the file in' guidance, fires a 12-second sonner toast, and crucially does NOT attempt the doomed URL-protocol launch nor wastes bandwidth on /api/exports/handoff staging. OrcaSlicer + PrusaSlicer (the iter-105.23 happy path) remain unaffected: staged handoff still POSTs and launchSlicer still runs.
